justification: National identity pride is inherently multi-dimensional, encompassing local belonging, explicit pride in being Mexican, and attachment to cultural traditions, so no single item captures the full construct. These three items share compatible ordinal Likert-style scales (4–8 categories) and all tap affective-evaluative attachment to national/local identity, making them combinable via mean score. p5_1a (open-ended emotion) and p7 (incomplete stem) are excluded due to ambiguous or open-ended coding that would introduce scale incompatibility and noise in aggregation.

Tolerance toward social diversity is multi-dimensional, spanning behavioral exposure (diverse friendships) and normative acceptance (same-sex adoption rights), so aggregation is warranted over any single item. PCA is preferred because the friendship-quantity items (p54_5, p54_6, p54_8, all K=5) and the agreement scale (p68, K=7) have somewhat different distributional shapes and the first principal component will optimally weight items by their shared variance in the latent tolerance dimension. p9 (open-ended opinion) and p73_1 (willingness to cohabit) are excluded because their coding is less standardized or conflates distinct behavioral intentions that may not load cleanly on a single tolerance factor.

Trust is a well-established multi-dimensional construct spanning generalized interpersonal trust and specific institutional trust, and all three available items are needed to represent both facets. However, p51_1 and p52_1 use 0–10 numeric scales (K=13) while p50_1 uses a 6-point Likert scale, making simple summation or mean-scoring scale-incompatible; PCA handles this by extracting the shared variance regardless of raw metric differences. The first principal component will capture the common latent trust disposition, and binning to 5 quantiles yields a well-distributed ordinal target suitable for the bridge model.

Personal optimism and efficacy is a broad construct encompassing future-project confidence, life satisfaction, happiness, and perceived control over one's future, requiring multiple items to cover its scope adequately. The items span two distinct scale families (7-point Likert: p20, p24; 0–10 numeric: p29, p74), making PCA the appropriate aggregation method to extract shared variance without distortion from raw metric differences. p19 (binary economic security confidence) and p30_1 (economic control, 6-point) are excluded to keep the item set focused on the psychological optimism-efficacy core rather than domain-specific economic perceptions, reducing construct contamination.

Moral-normative conservatism is a latent attitudinal disposition that manifests across family discipline norms, self-identified ideological orientation, gender role attitudes, sexual morality, and child-rearing values, requiring the full item battery to capture its breadth. The items use heterogeneous scales (5-point yes/no-style, 8-point self-placement, binary choice), so PCA is the only defensible aggregation method, extracting the dominant conservative-vs-liberal attitudinal axis from shared variance across all items. p66 is excluded because it is explicitly flagged as not present in the data, and including a missing variable would corrupt the factor solution.
